item.title: Is It Ever Morally Right to Hack? | Bridewell
item.description: Ethical Hacking is a term widely used to define IT Security professionals who attempt to break into IT systems to identify weaknesses. Find out more

item.title: Global Privacy in Practice: Lessons from 2025 and the Roadmap for 2026 | Bridewell
item.description: The start of a new year is time for reflection, reset and setting priorities. 2025 left a clear legacy: record-level fines, increasingly coordinated cross-border enforcement and growing focus on how privacy controls operate in practice.

item.title: Bridewell | CNI Cyber Security Summit Roundup
item.description: In this blog, will be sharing some of the key talking points from the CNI Cyber Security Summit April 2024.

item.title: Bridewell | What is an Agentic SOC? Understanding the Evolution Beyond Automation
item.description: What is an Agentic SOC? Understanding the Evolution Beyond Automation. When uptime is non-negotiable and regulatory scrutiny is intense, understanding what an agentic SOC actually delivers becomes essential.

item.title: Secure by Design, Delivered: A Practitioner’s Playbook for Government Teams | Bridewell
item.description: This blog distils Secure by Design down into actionable building blocks and provides guidance on how project teams can embed Secure by Design requirements into agile project delivery.

item.description: Who Needs to Be PCI DSS Compliant?

item.title: Microsoft Security Copilot and OpenAI ChatGPT: How Can AI Change the Future of Cyber Security?
item.description: AI has become a hot topic among cyber security professionals in 2023. This conversation has largely been driven by OpenAI’s ChatGPT, which has gained a lot of attention in the tech world for its broad capabilities.

item.description: Understanding the Data Use and Access Act 2025

item.description: In this blog, James Smith, Head of Offensive Security runs through the steps he took to identify a number of security issues that you should be aware of if you are using, or planning to use, these devices.

item.description: In this blog, we’ll look at what happened during the Coupang data breach as well as what other organisations can learn from how Coupang responded to the incident.

item.title: Looking At ISO 27001 Certification? Guidance On How To Avoid The Cowboys

item.description: In this blog, we provide an overview of the CAF and DSPT, explain who falls within the scope of the CAF-aligned DSPT, and outline the key submission milestones to help organisations prepare effectively.

item.description: In this blog, Will Smith, Academy Consultant, shares his insights and experiences from his time in the Bridewell Academy.

item.description: This month’s Threat Insights leverages data from Bridewell’s global MDR and Incident Response engagements to expose the top malicious infrastructure of April 2026.

item.title: PCI WHY: Why Should I Comply with PCI DSS?
item.description: In our latest blog, we dive into the key reasons your organiSation should consider PCI DSS compliance—even if it’s not being demanded right now. From protecting customer data to reducing the risk of breaches, the benefits are clear.

item.description: DragonForce is a relatively new threat actor first identified in 2023. Read our latest insights to discover the latest on the DragonForce Ransomware.

item.description: Preparing for a CAF assessment? Learn how to manage contributing outcomes, evidence, IGP scoring logic and QA with practical tips from an experienced CAF assessor.

item.description: The approach to cyber security needs to be driven from the top down with a Zero-Trust security strategy to help achieve the shared goals and be successful. Read on

item.description: Deploying AI within your organisation may seem simple but, if personal data is involved, its use will likely have a fundamental impact on your data privacy programme.

item.description: Read our round up of the CNI Cyber Security Summit 2025, hosted in central London on March 20th, facilitated a wide range of discussions about how we can work together to secure the future of UK CNI.

item.description: Understanding that your employees play a key role in ensuring the security of your computers and networks and why you need to train your staff about Cyber Security.

item.description: Instead of ‘getting GDPR compliant’ the emphasis should move towards a continual set of processes, procedures and supporting technology. Find out more

item.title: Threat Advisory: Bridewell Sounds the Alarm on New 'I'm Better' Malware Impersonating Popular Online Tools and Video Games
item.description: Bridewell Cyber Threat Intelligence has identified a previously unreported infostealer, named "I'm Better", that targets valuable information such as cryptocurrency wallets, browser credentials, and session cookies.

item.title: What is a Threat-Informed Defence Strategy?
item.description: In this blog, we’ll dive further into how a threat informed defence strategy differs from conventional approaches, how to implement it, and how it benefits your organisation.
